question: Top 10 pizza places in nyc

places.formattedAddress: 227 Malcolm X Blvd, New York, NY 10027, USA

text.text: Scottocasa Pizzeria in Harlem is an absolute gem! From the moment you walk in, you can taste the love and craftsmanship in every handmade pizza. The crust is perfection—light, crisp, and full of flavor—topped with the freshest ingredients and incredible balance. Every bite is unforgettable. The staff is just as amazing as the food: welcoming, attentive, and genuinely passionate about what they do. It’s not just pizza, it’s an experience. If you’re in Harlem and love authentic, handcrafted pizza with outstanding service, Scottocasa is a must-visit.

The staff is just as amazing as the food: welcoming, attentive, and genuinely passionate about what they do. It’s not just pizza, it’s an experience. If you’re in Harlem and love authentic, handcrafted pizza with outstanding service, Scottocasa is a must-visit.

text.text: THIS PIZZA!!! Seriously. The whole pie. I ate the whole thing. The crust is super thin and the char from the oven is chef's kiss. I had the margherita pizza and OMG!!! We had the burratta salad and yes to all of the fresh mozzarella. If you are in Harlem, you have to go here.  You will not be disappointed!

text.text:
Amazing vibe. This place was hopping, but not too loud, when most of Manhattan was too cold to leave the house.

Amazing salads and pizza with gluten free options. The calzone was yummy too!

Fantastic selection of beer and wine.

Friendly, attentive, and on point staff.

text.text: It’s good, but not sure if it’s worth the hype. I think it would be great if their tomato sauce had more robust flavor

originalText.text: It’s good, but not sure if it’s worth the hype. I think it would be great if their tomato sauce had more robust flavor

text.text:
Great Italian food. It was my first time trying authentic Italian food and it was better than I thought. The good focuses on the natural flavor of ingredients, so you will taste what you see. In overall, it seems like not much of seasoning was used, which I liked. Not greasy, fresh and light flavor, so even I could finish one pizza by myself. You will not get tired of pizza. The dough was also really good. Chewy but not hard.
If you are used to New York pizza style or salty food with lot of seasonings, this pizza could be bland to you, but I actually liked this kinds of style.
I got Verdure with Soppierssata added. I really enjoyed it and recommend it!

text.text:
Really crisp crust, loaded with filling, clean, and the best in East Village!

This time I had their new flavor with fresh burrsta and arugula on top at the end sprinkled eight olive oil!

I love the pepperoni with pesto and lots of cheese! Heaven!

This place have tables and chairs and good pizza! One of the best in the city!

Came to us hot and ready to eat. I like the doughiness and sweet sauce. Not a lot of cheese but an appropriate amount.

text.text: This is the best pizza i’ve ever had. New york style perfectly, the crust is amazing, no flop, great flavors and the staff is very friendly. It’s also very clean and nice vibe overall.

text.text: Had two slices delivered. Absolutely disgusting. The pizza was about the thickness of two paper plates together. Completely burnt. Every bite was burnt crunch. Can’t taste the cheese. Trash pizza. Never again.

editorialSummary.text: Modern outpost of a longtime counter-serve pizza joint prepping New York-style slices and pies.

text.text:
I just had to share my amazing experience at Joe’s Pizza — it truly exceeded all my expectations!

From the moment I walked in, the atmosphere was warm, lively, and welcoming. The staff greeted me with genuine smiles and were incredibly friendly and attentive throughout my visit. It’s clear they take pride not just in their food but in making customers feel valued and comfortable.

Now let’s talk about the pizza — absolutely phenomenal. The crust was perfectly crisp on the outside while staying soft and chewy inside. The sauce had that rich, balanced flavor that tastes fresh and homemade, and the cheese was melted to perfection with just the right stretch in every bite. Every slice was packed with flavor, and you can really tell they use high-quality ingredients.

Service was fast and efficient even though the place was busy, which says a lot about how well the team works together. The staff were patient, helpful with recommendations, and made the whole experience even better.

If you’re looking for authentic, delicious pizza served by a team that genuinely cares, Joe’s Pizza is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be coming back for more!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Highly recommend

text.text:
Joe’s Pizza (Broadway) is undeniably one of New York’s classic pizza institutions and often one of the first recommendations for anyone visiting the city. Whether you’re grabbing a quick slice or ordering a whole pie, it’s a convenient and iconic stop.

The prices remain reasonable, especially considering the location and popularity. However, be prepared for long queues—waiting times can easily range from 30 to 45 minutes, and sometimes even up to an hour. The line does move, but occasionally slower than expected.

Their classic cheese, Margherita, and pepperoni slices are the clear favorites—simple, well-executed, and consistently satisfying. Inside, you’ll also find plenty of photos featuring celebrities and famous visitors, which adds to the nostalgic charm and history of the place.

While there are certainly other pizza spots in New York that may offer more innovative or elevated flavors, Joe’s stands out for its legacy, convenience, and reliability. It’s less about being the absolute “best” and more about experiencing a true NYC staple.

Definitely worth adding to your to-do list when visiting New York—especially if you want a taste of pizza history.

text.text:
The perfect place to truly feel New York City. There are almost always lines, but they move fast and are absolutely worth the wait. Joe’s Pizza offers both thin and slightly thicker crust, so everyone can find something they like. At the counter you’ll find many different flavors, all of them consistently good — from classic cheese to more loaded options.

Slices are heated fresh on the spot, so you always get hot, crispy pizza with great flavor. The service is quick and efficient, even late at night, which makes it a perfect stop after a Broadway musical or an evening walk through the city.

This is not a fancy place — and that’s exactly the point. It’s loud, busy, authentic, and full of NYC energy. Joe’s Pizza is a true New York classic and an excellent choice for a quick, satisfying bite any time of day.

text.text: FANTASTIC low cost pizza!!! This it's my one must hit place in new york and I'm so glad I did. The pizza is affordable and it's light, crispy and delicious. There is definitely a wait to get in, but it's all part of the New York experience!!! I will say that their deep dish pizza was not my favorite and I would not get that again. However, if I return to New York I would definitely, make it a point to go here again.  The staff is friendly and the amount of pizza that they turn out is unreal!!! There is limited seating, so don't be upset if you don't get a seat.

text.text:
4.4 / 5 for a solid NY pie. Flavorful, tasty and staff were great.

We got a whole pepperoni fresh on a Saturday night, ready in 12 mins or so. Looked good, sauce was nice and they threw in a nice side of hot chilli flakes highly recommend).

This is classic New York. I will say it was not crunchy at all (the cup and char pepperoni were nice), but the dough and undercarriage had great color but not crunch. Missing that made it a 4.4 for me.

But I would go back, and the staff were polite, easy to work with wanted me to have a great experience.

editorialSummary.text: Upper West Side pizzeria popular for slices with classic and gourmet toppings.

text.text:
We had so many options to choose from for pizza in New York, but we decided to give Mama's Too a try as we had heard good things. We tried one of the square slices. They kindly cut it in half for us as it is quite a large slice. We tried the vodka sauce slice, and it was amazing. Crispy on the bottom, saucy on top! That vodka sauce is stupendous. I know it isn't a traditional NY slice but this was one of our favorite pizzas of our trip! Staff were friendly and efficient. The place was quite busy, but we got our food quickly. It is standing counters only here. I would definitely recommend!! We will make sure we come back next time we are in New York!

Just as a note there is no public restroom here

text.text: Phenomenal. They do a great Sicilian dough. The cacio e Pepe slice is a genius way to elevate a white slice into something truly exciting. The sausage slice with ricotta and peppers was a wonderful balance of flavors. The vodka slice was flavorful without being too rich and the fresh basil on top is lovely. Their traditional slices are also top notch, they do a great regular.

The sausage slice with ricotta and peppers was a wonderful balance of flavors. The vodka slice was flavorful without being too rich and the fresh basil on top is lovely. Their traditional slices are also top notch, they do a great regular.

text.text: Built up an appetite last weekend with a walk through Central Park and finally made it to Mamma’s Too on the UWS. Oh wow—so worth it! Definitely in my top five NYC pizza spots. Their pizzas check all the boxes: perfectly crispy crust, fresh and flavorful ingredients, and creative topping combos that totally work. I tried the hot honey pepperoni, cacio e pepe, and the bruschetta—all three were amazing and bursting with flavor. I’ll definitely be back soon to try more of their slices.

I tried the hot honey pepperoni, cacio e pepe, and the bruschetta—all three were amazing and bursting with flavor. I’ll definitely be back soon to try more of their slices.

text.text:
Is this place worth the hype? Short answer Hell Yah!
Really good pizza slices - but the sandwich’s steel the show! The best Cheese steak I e ever had and I’ve been to Philly up there with Angelos! If you know you know ! It was so good I forgot to get a picture !
I only wish we went to to other location - this location doesn’t have seating- but I’m still giving a 5 star - so good the chicken Parm was excellent sandwich as well, but the cheesesteak is next level!!!

text.text: Living in NYC we sure are spoiled when it comes to pizza and Mamas TOO is no exception. Excellent flavor, quality ingredients and now the ability to eat your pizza on a small counter? Love it. We had the angry Nonna, Original, upside down. Already discussing what to get next time! Counter guys were fantastic.

editorialSummary.text: Relaxed Italian restaurant dishing up traditional fare like pasta, brick-oven pizzas & desserts.

text.text: My very first night in NYC and I am lucky enough to be staying right across the street from this most excellent eatery. Tonight I had the daily special pizza accompanied by the house wine. The whole menu is appetizing. Even as a solo diner I received prompt, courteous service. The other tables of customers were all in high spirits and it’s no doubt to me it is just the vibe here. I especially enjoy that I feel welcome to linger over my wine.  I ❤️NY and Bosino Brick Oven Pizza

text.text: Very nice neighborhood place; I had the Bosino pizza and it was very good (maybe a little bit too “rich”/cheesy for me but I still ate everything…); clients seem to be regulars and Salvatore takes very good care of them; I had a lovely dinner outside and felt like part of the family 🙂 Grazie mille, Salvatore !

text.text: I had such a great experience at Dados Pizza in Harlem! The pizza was fantastic, fresh, flavorful, and exactly what you want when you’re craving a good slice. What really stood out though was the customer service. The staff was incredibly kind and welcoming, and they went above and beyond to make me feel appreciated. They even gave me T-shirts for my kids at home. Kara, I believe was her name, was so kind and happy at the register! The shirts totally made their day when I got home and turned a simple pizza stop for me into a memorable story. We’ll definitely be back when we visit NYC again!

text.text: 1$ Wednesday wings are really good! The best flavor is teriyaki!!! Their pizza is also very tasty and they do have a variety of toppings! Their pizza service is okay, they have their good days and their bad days!

originalText.text: 1$ Wednesday wings are really good! The best flavor is teriyaki!!! Their pizza is also very tasty and they do have a variety of toppings! Their pizza service is okay, they have their good days and their bad days!

text.text: Dado’s Pizza Wow! Hands down one of the best slices I've had in NYC. Dado's in Harlem nails that perfect thin crust-crispy, flavorful, and just the right amount of char. Super friendly staff, great beer selection, and a chill vibe that makes you want to stay. This place is a gem.

originalText.text: Dado’s Pizza Wow! Hands down one of the best slices I've had in NYC. Dado's in Harlem nails that perfect thin crust-crispy, flavorful, and just the right amount of char. Super friendly staff, great beer selection, and a chill vibe that makes you want to stay. This place is a gem.

text.text: Never ordering from here again. First of all it’s overpriced, and I gave too much of a tip at the start for the poor service i ended up receiving. I was told it would be 20 minutes, it ended up being double that. No heads up about it whatsoever. Once I finally got my pizza and made it home about 5 minutes later, all the cheese slid off to one side of the pizza as I held it as leveled as I could as I walked home with it. I’d rather eat from dominos pizza that’s a few blocks away honestly. It’s cheaper, more bang for your buck, tastes better, and I don’t have to worry about the cheese immediately sliding off. I don’t get the hype for Dado’s pizza at all.

text.text: Cool place. Slices were tasty. We tried the chicken bacon ranch (a little heavy on the ranch), sausage and peppers (good amount of pepper), cheese, and pepperoni. Also had the Caesar salad which was yummy. They had 3 beers on tap and a bunch of cans. Great selection. We had one from Grimm and one from Other Half.

text.text:
Nice slice, pepperoni and sausage was on point. Lots and lots of options, I didn't get the square pie with the cupping pepperoni but it looked delish.

Prices are good, place is clean, lots of pizza options in town but can't go wrong here.

text.text: Was excited and eager to come and taste a slice or 2 of NYC style pizza. Lets just say, this exceeded my expectations. I do have to mention, the chicken nugget one isn't my fave but the veggie one and cheese were definitely good.

originalText.text: Was excited and eager to come and taste a slice or 2 of NYC style pizza. Lets just say, this exceeded my expectations. I do have to mention, the chicken nugget one isn't my fave but the veggie one and cheese were definitely good.

text.text: Had the vodka and Margherita pizzas and they were delicious, I especially loved the crust - thinner but a little chewy and crispy. The outside dining area is a nice bonus. Definitely will be back!

originalText.text: Had the vodka and Margherita pizzas and they were delicious, I especially loved the crust - thinner but a little chewy and crispy. The outside dining area is a nice bonus. Definitely will be back!

text.text:
🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

Hands down, the BEST New York-style pizza I’ve had in a long time!
“Made in New York Pizza” delivers everything you’d want in an authentic NYC slice—crispy, thin crust with that perfect fold, rich and flavorful sauce, and just the right amount of cheese. The dough tastes fresh, the toppings are top quality, and you can tell they take real pride in every pie they make.

The atmosphere has that classic, no-frills NYC pizzeria vibe—quick, friendly service, clean space, and the smell alone is enough to make your mouth water the second you walk in. I grabbed a pepperoni and a white slice, and both were absolutely 🔥🔥🔥.

Whether you’re a tourist wanting the real NYC pizza experience or a local looking for your go-to spot, this place is a must-visit. Trust me, one slice won’t be enough. Can’t wait to come back!

📸 As someone who runs a creative agency focused on photography, videography, and marketing, I can say this spot is photogenic and flavorful.

If you’re a restaurant looking to level up your online presence, highlight your menu with mouth-watering visuals, or capture the soul of your brand on video — I’d love to collaborate! DM me or visit my page to see how we can make your food and business stand out in a crowded digital world. 🍕🎥📱

text.text:
Another hidden gem. Not a tourist trap.

Randomly came here while waiting for the bus. So glad we came to this unassuming place. Somehow we’ll have to make the trip uptown again just for this.

Really amazing slice of buratta and margherita grandma.

places.formattedAddress: 278 Bleecker St, New York, NY 10014, USA

editorialSummary.text: Brick-oven pies (no slices) with crisp thin crusts are the signature of this circa-1929 pizzeria.

text.text: Very friendly staffs and the store vibe feels very cool for tourist like me! I had so much fun looking at photos on the wall, it was like seeing history! Pizza did not disappoint! I normally prefer simple cheese pizza but here, pepperoni was superior! The hint of spice in the pepperoni paired so well with the simple base. Definitely worth the visit if u like pizza.

I normally prefer simple cheese pizza but here, pepperoni was superior! The hint of spice in the pepperoni paired so well with the simple base. Definitely worth the visit if u like pizza.

text.text: Loved this place! The service was great! That coal fired oven and years of experience add up to some incredible pizza! Loved the whole place. We got the john's original on half and added kalamata olives to the other side. There was a line to get in, but it moved quickly and was worth the short wait!

We got the john's original on half and added kalamata olives to the other side. There was a line to get in, but it moved quickly and was worth the short wait!

text.text: Really good garlic bread, some of the best!  The cheese sticks are ok, average but the dip is delicious and the pizza is sol good.  I think my favourite in new york.  Their reputation is well deserved.

originalText.text: Really good garlic bread, some of the best!  The cheese sticks are ok, average but the dip is delicious and the pizza is sol good.  I think my favourite in new york.  Their reputation is well deserved.

text.text: we came for a slice of authentic nyc pizza, that odd ball “one bite you know the rules” guy rated this place highly, so it was on our list. waited outside in the cold for 30 minutes, once we got inside and so I just have small and tightly packed the places we understood why. No complaints, pizza was great, seeing the guys in the kitchen, hustling pizzas out, and watching their system was entertaining and impressive. Would go back, can see why it’s popular. Definitely helped make our nyc experience with the kids compete

text.text:
John's of Bleecker Street absolutely lives up to its reputation. The pizza is classic, no-nonsense, and incredibly satisfying—thin crust with just the right balance of crisp and chew. The sauce is bright and flavorful, tasting fresh and well-seasoned without overpowering the pie, and the cheese melts perfectly into every slice.

What really stands out is the consistency and simplicity. There’s nothing flashy here, just expertly made pizza that shows why this place has been an institution for so long. It’s the kind of spot you leave already thinking about when you’ll come back for another slice.

places.formattedAddress: 235 Mulberry St, New York, NY 10012, USA

editorialSummary.text: Casual spot serving reinvented Italian-American classics and thin-crust pizzas, including the standout Tie Dye pizza, in a cozy space.

text.text:
Fun, quintessential little Italy vibes of an established New York City restaurant. Great service, and menu is much brighter than just pizza. Braised beef braciole and short rib anglotti were favorites for our table.
Reservations took extensive planning and setting a timer at midnight to jump on it, but totally worth it. We went on a cold day where wait times were short, but I don’t think this is typical.
Waiter was particularly attentive to our food, allergies, and double checked with the kitchen on ingredients.
The menu is not overwhelming and there’s a focused list of desserts and beverages. The kind of place where options have been carefully honed over years of service.
We also tried the tie-dye and honey pie pizzas. Crust was exceptionally thin. This is what New York style pizza is traditionally known for. If this is your thing, you’ll love it. They have it down to a science.
A definite repeat. Would rate 7.5/10 for the pizza experience, but all else was a solid 9+.

text.text:
When in New York you have to get pizza right… well I think they need to clarify when they say that and tell you to get Rubirosa’s pizza. Came here on a recommendation, risked the long wait, and man was it worth it. We sat at the bar and were helped by Sergio! Ordered two pizzas. One was a traditional fresco. And the other a half and half gluten free with honey pie and tie dye. The gluten free was phenomenal. They don’t make their crust in house but it is from a local establishment. The vodka sauce on their tie dye is impeccable. I loved that the crust was crispy! I devoured it all. The half and half is the way to go!!! All of that said, when in New York get pizza. But make sure it is Rubirosa’s!!

Knocking it down and star because of cost- gluten free is smaller than non but still same cost if not more…

text.text: It is a rustic yet homey restaurant serving traditional Italian fares. Their thin crust pizza is crispy and chewy, and pasta is fresh with rich garlic savory pasta sauce.  Their zappoli is perfectly fried crispy outside and doughy soft inside 🙂, and cocktails are delicious!  It is a popular go to place and is crowded. So, you should make a reservation to avoid the long wait

So, you should make a reservation to avoid the long wait

text.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Rubirosa is pure comfort, warmth, and excellence from start to finish.

From the moment we walked in, the ambiance wrapped us up like being in a beloved grandma’s house — cozy, inviting, nostalgic, and full of heart. It’s the kind of place that instantly makes you relax and feel taken care of.

The service was truly exceptional. Our server, Alyssa, was warm, attentive, and wonderful, but honestly the entire team went above and beyond. You can feel how much pride they take in what they do, and that energy carries through the whole experience.

And then… the food. Absolutely outstanding. We started with the fried calamari — perfectly crispy and flavorful. The Supreme pizza and Bianca pizza were both phenomenal, each bite better than the last. Even the bread was delicious (which says a lot). We finished with the tiramisu, which was rich, balanced, and the perfect ending.

The espresso martinis deserve their own mention — smooth, rich, perfectly balanced, and honestly some of the best I’ve had. The ideal complement to an already incredible meal.

But the moment that really sealed it? They brought out a mini pizza box filled with mini chocolate slices. Thoughtful, playful, and unforgettable.

Everything was truly delectable. Rubirosa is one of those rare places that gets everything right — atmosphere, service, drinks, and food. I will absolutely be coming back, even if it’s out of my way.

Highly, highly recommend.

text.text: Wow this meal was unreal. Everything hit the mark. No joke the pizza has me looking up Rubirosa sauce jars to order because the pizza was that good. Great arugula salad with the tastiest dressing and an absolutely delicious ravioli dish in the butter sauce. I don’t *want* to come back… I NEED to come back. ❤️

editorialSummary.text: Huge slices cut from giant pies are budget-priced at this pizza joint that stays open late.

text.text:
Hugeeeeee slices - bigger than you would expect!!

We got the eggplant parm and vodka sauce pizza, we really enjoyed! All condiments are available including hot sauce, garlic, crushed red peppers, etc. Big pizza selection including many vegetarian options. The eggplant parm pizza is a bit on the sweeter side and vodka one is very savory. Happy we tried Koronet!

text.text: Fast service. Plenty of choice. The jumbo slices are really huge and 1 normal slice was enough for me. My wife couldn’t finish her jumbo slice. No public restroom.

originalText.text: Fast service. Plenty of choice. The jumbo slices are really huge and 1 normal slice was enough for me. My wife couldn’t finish her jumbo slice. No public restroom.

text.text:
Koronet Pizza
2848 Broadway
New York, NY 10025

Allá Vodka with Basil Jumbo Slice

What drew me to this urban pizza is the enormous size of their slices.  It’s definitely a unique experience being served a whopping 16” slice that envelopes the entire tray!

#koronetpizza #jumboslice #allavodkawithbasil

text.text:
Koronet Pizza – The BIGGEST & BEST Slice in NYC 🍕🔥

Let me tell you Koronet Pizza has the largest slices I’ve ever seen in New York City 😭🍕! Like literally, one slice could feed you for breakfast, lunch, and dinner (and I’m not exaggerating lol).

I ordered a cheesy slice, and the flavor was everything perfectly crisp on the edges, soft in the middle, and loaded with that real NY pizza taste. It was so good that I’m already craving another one today.

The vibe inside feels classic New York chill, cozy, and unpretentious. It’s the kind of spot that makes you feel like you’re in on one of the city’s best-kept secrets.

If you’re in the mood for that true NYC slice experience go to Koronet Pizza. Come hungry… because these slices are next-level huge and absolutely delicious 😋🍕

#KoronetPizza #NYCPizza #FoodieFinds #BiggestSliceInNYC #PizzaLovers #NYCEats #Ciciamoreats

text.text:
Several options, speedy service and best of all: delicious.

There was a decent crowd when I went in, but I had virtually no wait before I had a slice in hand. I also went back on a Saturday afternoon to order a slice as big as my head, and it did not disappoint. I'd recommend sharing it with a friend, but it's delicious on your own as well.

Compared to other pizza by the slice, I think Koronet has the best crust and sauce, putting it a slice above the rest for a similar cost. Underrated and delicious.

final_answer:
Oh, you're looking for the best pizza in the city? You've come to the right place! NYC has some truly iconic pizza, and I've found a few highly-rated spots that are definitely worth checking out.

Here are 7 fantastic pizza places in NYC:

*   **Sottocasa Pizzeria Harlem**
    *   **Address:** 227 Malcolm X Blvd, New York, NY 10027, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.7 stars (1524 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Moderate
    *   **Phone:** (646) 928-4870
    *   **Website:** http://www.sottocasanyc.com/harlem/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 10:00 PM on Sunday.

*   **Nuovo York Pizza**
    *   **Address:** 105 E 9th St, New York, NY 10003, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.8 stars (1574 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Moderate
    *   **Phone:** (646) 476-2428
    *   **Website:** https://www.nuovoyorkpizza.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 3:00 AM on Monday (technically 3:00 AM on Tuesday for today's hours, but the next close time for Sunday is 4:30 AM).

*   **Joe's Pizza Broadway**
    *   **Address:** 1435 Broadway, New York, NY 10018, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.5 stars (24700 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Inexpensive
    *   **Phone:** (646) 559-4878
    *   **Website:** https://www.joespizzanyc.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 3:00 AM on Monday (next close time for Sunday is 5:00 AM).

*   **Mama's TOO! Upper West Side**
    *   **Address:** 2750 Broadway, New York, NY 10025, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.2 stars (2672 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Inexpensive
    *   **Phone:** (212) 510-7256
    *   **Website:** http://mamastoo.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 11:00 PM on Sunday.

*   **Bosino**
    *   **Address:** 201 W 103rd St, New York, NY 10025, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.9 stars (454 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Moderate
    *   **Phone:** (646) 755-8770
    *   **Website:** http://bosinonyc.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 10:00 PM on Sunday.

*   **Dado's Pizza and Taproom Harlem**
    *   **Address:** 341 St Nicholas Ave, New York, NY 10027, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.7 stars (354 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Moderate
    *   **Phone:** (646) 547-1414
    *   **Website:** http://dadospizza.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 10:00 PM on Sunday.

*   **Made In New York Pizza**
    *   **Address:** 421 Amsterdam Ave, New York, NY 10024, USA
    *   **Rating:** 4.6 stars (1242 reviews)
    *   **Price Level:** Inexpensive
    *   **Phone:** (646) 484-6200
    *   **Website:** https://www.madeinnewyorkpizza.com/
    *   **Status:** Open now. Closes at 11:00 PM on Sunday.

These spots offer a great variety, from classic New York slices to gourmet options. Joe's Pizza is a true classic, while places like Sottocasa and Bosino are highly rated for their authentic Italian-style pies. If you're looking for something with a unique twist, Mama's TOO! is known for its creative slices!

Enjoy your pizza adventure! Let me know if you want directions to any of these places.
